# Vendor Standards

Ready White uses subcontractor fulfillment through standardized packages, preset buy rates, photo proof, and scorecards. Do not build the vendor system around flat hourly labor.

## Vendor Operating Model

Vendors accept work by:

- Package buy rate.
- Room size band.
- Standard add-ons.
- Approved exception scope.

Hourly/change-order rates are only for severe prep, water damage, smoke/stain blocking, custom repairs, or approved scope exceptions.

## Required Vendor Capabilities

- Fast response to dispatch requests.
- Ability to follow standardized scopes.
- Photo proof submission before completion approval.
- Clean property turnover behavior suitable for property managers.
- Callback correction process.
- Reliability across vacant turnover timelines.

## Scorecard

Track each vendor by:

| Metric | Why it matters |
| --- | --- |
| Response time | Protects speed-to-lead and scheduling speed. |
| On-time start | Protects vacancy turnover time. |
| On-time completion | Protects PM relationships. |
| Photo proof compliance | Protects QA and customer trust. |
| Callback rate | Protects margins and vendor ranking. |
| Scope adherence | Prevents operational drift. |
| Communication quality | Reduces coordinator load. |

## Enforcement

- One missed photo-proof requirement: coach and require correction before completion approval.
- Repeated missing proof: lower assignment priority.
- Missed SLA without notice: assign backup vendor and flag scorecard.
- Callback caused by scope noncompliance: vendor correction required before future preferred assignments.
- Repeated quality misses: remove from preferred bench.

## Vendor Bench Redundancy

Each active market should maintain:

- At least one primary vendor.
- At least one backup vendor.
- Clear package buy rates for both.
- Availability notes for vacant-turnover urgency.
- A current scorecard review date.
